conforms_to: gnuin_compose_core::{diff, damage, SceneDiff, NodeChange} — see compose_core.rs / src/diff.rs
thesis: retained-mode reactivity: state → reduce → new Scene → diff → targeted host updates. The host keeps the previous Scene and applies only the change set; damage() gives the dirty region so a motion-only restyle never forces a full-screen repaint (E5).

rules.Added: id in next, not in prev
rules.Removed: id in prev, not in next
rules.Updated: same id, same rect, but state/z/tokens/payload changed
rules.move_plus_restyle: reports BOTH Moved and Updated (separate entries)
rules.damage: union of changed rects — next rect, plus prev rect when moved or removed; Rect::ZERO when nothing changed

cases.name: move menu (200,200)→(900,600)
cases.note: no Added/Removed — same id on both sides

cases.name: perf tier flip Sdf→Static (same rect)
cases.note: Updated, NOT Moved — motion/restyle in place; damage is the node rect, far smaller than the 1920×1080 screen (E5)

cases.name: move + restyle together
cases.note: both changes reported as separate entries
